PATTOOL_PARAMS.TITLE: PATTOOL Parameters
PATTOOL_PARAMS.SUBTITLE: Server configuration console — read-only, secrets masked
PATTOOL_PARAMS.LOAD_ERROR: Unable to load parameters. Admin role required.

DESC.EXTRA_PROPERTY: Property present in application.properties but not listed in the curated catalog; effect depends on Spring or custom code reading this key.
DESC.MONGO_RUNTIME: Runtime value stored in MongoDB appParameters
DESC.UNKNOWN: No detailed description is available for this parameter key.

FILTER.NO_RESULTS: No parameters match the current filters.

PARAM.spring_application_name: Internal Spring Boot application name used in logs, health checks and service identification.
PARAM.server_port: TCP port on which the PatTool API listens for HTTP requests (Angular frontend calls this port).
PARAM.spring_data_mongodb_host: MongoDB server hostname when not using a full connection URI (events, members, appParameters, etc.).
PARAM.spring_data_mongodb_port: MongoDB TCP port (default 27017) used with spring.data.mongodb.host.
PARAM.spring_data_mongodb_database: MongoDB database name where PatTool stores all collections (evenements, members, discussions…).
PARAM.spring_data_mongodb_uri: Full MongoDB connection string; when set, it typically overrides host/port and may embed credentials.
PARAM.spring_servlet_multipart_max_file_size: Maximum size of a single uploaded file (photos, videos, attachments) accepted by Spring.
PARAM.spring_servlet_multipart_max_request_siz: Maximum total size of a multipart HTTP request (all files + fields combined).
PARAM.app_uploaddir: Temporary directory on the server where uploaded files are written before processing or storage.
PARAM.file_storage_base_path: Root folder on disk for persisted event images and related media served by PatTool.
PARAM.app_imagemaxsizekb: Threshold (KB) above which the UI may prompt users to compress images before upload.
PARAM.keycloak_realm: Keycloak realm name used to validate JWT tokens and resolve user roles for PatTool.
PARAM.keycloak_auth_server_url: Base URL of the Keycloak server (login, token, admin endpoints) trusted by the backend.
PARAM.keycloak_resource: Keycloak client/resource identifier configured for the backend resource server adapter.
PARAM.keycloak_bearer_only: When true, the API accepts only bearer tokens and does not initiate browser login flows itself.
PARAM.keycloak_client_id: OAuth2 client id used by PatTool when talking to Keycloak (often matches the frontend client).
PARAM.keycloak_credentials_secret: Client secret for confidential Keycloak clients; required for server-side token validation.
PARAM.app_cors_allowed_origins: Comma-separated list of frontend origins allowed to call the API with credentials (CORS).
PARAM.pat_security_awareness_scanner_dashboard: Link shown on the Security Awareness page to your internal vulnerability scanner dashboard.
PARAM.pat_security_awareness_internal_runbook_: Link to an internal security runbook/playbook referenced from the Security Awareness tools.
PARAM.pat_passive_probe_allow_private_targets: Allows the admin passive HTTP probe to target private/LAN IPs (disabled by default for safety).
PARAM.pat_passive_probe_max_redirects: Maximum number of HTTP redirects followed when running a passive probe.
PARAM.pat_passive_probe_connect_timeout_second: TCP connect timeout for passive probe outbound HTTP calls.
PARAM.pat_passive_probe_request_timeout_second: Overall read/request timeout for passive probe HTTP responses.
PARAM.app_mailsentfrom: From address used when PatTool sends outbound emails (reminders, shares, notifications).
PARAM.app_mailsentto: Default recipient address for server-triggered emails when no explicit recipient is provided.
PARAM.app_sendmail: Master switch to enable or disable all outbound mail sending from the backend.
PARAM.app_connection_email_enabled: Sends an email to admins when a user signs in (connection notification).
PARAM.app_connection_email_min_interval_minute: Minimum minutes between duplicate connection-notification emails for the same user.
PARAM.assistant_provider: Default AI provider for the in-app assistant: openai, anthropic, gemini or mistral.
PARAM.assistant_billing_openai_billing_url: External link opened from the assistant UI to manage OpenAI billing.
PARAM.assistant_billing_openai_usage_url: External link to OpenAI usage dashboard shown in assistant billing hints.
PARAM.assistant_billing_anthropic_url: External link to Anthropic plans/billing page from the assistant UI.
PARAM.assistant_billing_gemini_rate_limit_url: External link explaining Gemini rate limits, shown to users in the assistant.
PARAM.assistant_billing_gemini_api_keys_url: External link to Google AI Studio / API keys page for Gemini setup.
PARAM.assistant_billing_mistral_url: External link to Mistral AI billing console, shown in the assistant.
PARAM.openai_api: Base URL for OpenAI chat/completions API calls made by OpenAiAssistantService.
PARAM.openai_key: Secret API key authenticating PatTool to OpenAI for assistant and billing calls.

CHIMIE.TITLE: Chemistry — elements & molecules
CHIMIE.SUBTITLE: Explore the periodic table and visualize molecules in realistic 3D. Data from PubChem (NIH), proxied server-side.
CHIMIE.COMPARE_INTRO: Load two compounds to compare their 3D structures and properties side by side.
CHIMIE.VIEWER_EMPTY: Search a molecule to display its 3D structure.
CHIMIE.RX_HINT: Use element symbols and '=' or '->' between reactants and products, e.g. CH4 + O2 = CO2 + H2O.
CHIMIE.RX_ERR_FORMAT: Use a single '=' (or '->') with reactants on the left and products on the right.
CHIMIE.RX_ERR_PARSE: Could not read one of the formulas. Check element symbols (e.g. H2O, C6H12O6).
CHIMIE.RX_ERR_BALANCE: This equation cannot be balanced (check the formulas and that all species are present).
CHIMIE.ERROR_ELEMENTS: Unable to load the periodic table right now.
CHIMIE.ERROR_MOLECULE: Unable to load this molecule right now.
CHIMIE.ERROR_NOT_FOUND: No compound found for this name. Try another spelling or an English name.
CHIMIE.VIEW_3D_HINT: Drag to rotate, scroll to zoom. Atoms use CPK colours.
CHIMIE.NO_3D: No 3D conformer available for this compound — see the 2D structure.

WORLD_SECURITY_SCAN.TITLE: Security posture & authorized testing scope
WORLD_SECURITY_SCAN.SUBTITLE: Legitimate security practice only: harden your PatTool deployment, learn defensive basics, and document written authorization before any assessment.
WORLD_SECURITY_SCAN.DISCLAIMER_BODY: PatTool does not scan arbitrary websites or attack servers. Use authorized tooling only on systems you own or where you have explicit written permission. Unauthorized testing is illegal.
WORLD_SECURITY_SCAN.PATTOOL_INTRO: Use this checklist as you operate your own PatTool stack (Spring Boot API + Angular + Keycloak + MongoDB).
WORLD_SECURITY_SCAN.PATTOOL_ARCH_1: Review Spring Security and JWT rules (SecurityConfig.java): keep defaults restrictive and prefer authenticated APIs.
WORLD_SECURITY_SCAN.PATTOOL_ARCH_2: Protect Keycloak: strong passwords, HTTPS in production, minimal realm/client roles, rotate secrets.
WORLD_SECURITY_SCAN.PATTOOL_ARCH_3: MongoDB: authentication enabled, network isolation, backups encrypted at rest where possible.
WORLD_SECURITY_SCAN.PATTOOL_ARCH_4: Secrets stay out of git: copy application.properties.example → application.properties locally; never commit credentials.
WORLD_SECURITY_SCAN.PATTOOL_ARCH_5: Frontend environment files must point to trusted API and auth endpoints only.
WORLD_SECURITY_SCAN.PATTOOL_OPS_1: Apply OS and JVM patches regularly; restart during maintenance windows.
WORLD_SECURITY_SCAN.PATTOOL_OPS_2: Run dependency scans (e.g. mvn dependency-check, npm audit) and upgrade risky libraries.
WORLD_SECURITY_SCAN.PATTOOL_OPS_3: Keep TLS certificates valid; enable HSTS only where HTTPS is correctly terminated.
WORLD_SECURITY_SCAN.PATTOOL_OPS_4: Centralize logs and monitor auth failures, upload anomalies, and disk usage.
WORLD_SECURITY_SCAN.PATTOOL_OPS_5: Exercise restore drills from Mongo/file backups at least yearly.
WORLD_SECURITY_SCAN.EDU_INTRO: Modern applications face predictable classes of flaws (injection, broken access control, misconfiguration). Understanding them helps you defend rather than attack.
WORLD_SECURITY_SCAN.EDU_RESP_1: If you discover a vulnerability in someone else's system, contact their security team or use their published disclosure policy.
WORLD_SECURITY_SCAN.EDU_RESP_2: Do not access, modify, or exfiltrate data beyond what is necessary to demonstrate impact.
WORLD_SECURITY_SCAN.EDU_RESP_3: Allow reasonable time for remediation before any public discussion.
WORLD_SECURITY_SCAN.EDU_LEGAL: Laws such as the CFAA (US), Computer Misuse Act (UK), and similar statutes elsewhere prohibit unauthorized access regardless of intent.
WORLD_SECURITY_SCAN.SCOPE_INTRO: Record scope before commissioning tests. Buttons below appear only when your administrator configures optional URLs on the server.
WORLD_SECURITY_SCAN.SCOPE_FETCH_ERROR: Could not load server links (sign in and ensure the API is reachable). You can still fill and copy the manifest locally.
WORLD_SECURITY_SCAN.SCOPE_DOMAINS_HINT: One entry per line. Restrict to staging mirrors when possible.
WORLD_SECURITY_SCAN.SCOPE_CONFIRM: I confirm written authorization exists for the listed targets within the stated period.
WORLD_SECURITY_SCAN.COPY_FAIL: Could not copy — confirm the checkbox or retry.
WORLD_SECURITY_SCAN.SCOPE_STORAGE_NOTE: This form is not stored by PatTool; copy the manifest into your ticket system or vault.
WORLD_SECURITY_SCAN.MANIFEST_FOOTER: Signer acknowledgement captured outside PatTool (ticket reference, PDF mandate, etc.).
WORLD_SECURITY_SCAN.PROBE_INTRO: Runs non-intrusive checks from the PatTool server (headers, HTTPS, certificates, security.txt). Optionally enable active checks: OPTIONS (Allow), TRACE probe, and /robots.txt — still bounded and authorization-required; not penetration testing. LAN targets may need pat.passive-probe.allow-private-targets=true.
WORLD_SECURITY_SCAN.PROBE_AUTH_CHECK: I confirm I am authorized to run these checks against this URL.
WORLD_SECURITY_SCAN.PROBE_ACTIVE_HINT: Active checks send extra HTTP requests to the same host you entered. They do not inject payloads or crawl the application.
WORLD_SECURITY_SCAN.PROBE_NEED_AUTH_URL: Enter a URL and confirm authorization.
WORLD_SECURITY_SCAN.PROBE_HTTP_ERROR: Request failed. Sign in and ensure the API is running.

SOLAR_SYSTEM.TITLE: Solar system map
SOLAR_SYSTEM.SUBTITLE: J2000 ecliptic-plane view: body positions relative to your chosen center (Sun, planet, Moon). Computed locally with astronomy-engine.
SOLAR_SYSTEM.LOG_SCALE: Logarithmic radial scale (easier inner ↔ outer layout)
SOLAR_SYSTEM.VIEW_HINT: The diagram is centered on your reference body. In-plane angles keep relative directions; radius uses adaptive mapping (see logarithmic option).
SOLAR_SYSTEM.MOON_FROM_EARTH: Moon — geocentric illumination (as seen from Earth)
SOLAR_SYSTEM.TIMELINE_HINT: Drag the slider to move backward or forward in time within the selected span. The window recenters if the date leaves the range (playback or manual date).
SOLAR_SYSTEM.SHOW_ROTATION_AXES: Rotation axes (IAU north pole, ecliptic projection)
SOLAR_SYSTEM.ROTATION_AXIS_HINT: Line + arrow: projected north pole direction; dashed circle: axis nearly perpendicular to the plane.
SOLAR_SYSTEM.VIEW_3D_HINT: Drag to orbit, scroll to zoom. Same center and J2000 ecliptic frame as the flat map (sphere sizes are stylized).

CIEL.TITLE: Sky map
CIEL.SUBTITLE: Embedded Stellarium Web planetarium. Geolocation and object search go through the PatTool backend (Noctua Sky API).

WORLD_GLOBE.ISS_COMPASS_OPEN: Where to look for the ISS
WORLD_GLOBE.ISS_COMPASS_OPEN_HINT: Open a compass showing the direction and angle to look toward to spot the ISS from your location.
WORLD_GLOBE.ISS_COMPASS_TITLE: Where to look for the ISS
WORLD_GLOBE.ISS_COMPASS_NO_GEO: Location unavailable. Allow location access, or click a point on the globe to set your position.
WORLD_GLOBE.ISS_COMPASS_NO_SENSOR: Compass sensors unavailable: North is shown at the top. Hold the device flat and read the bearing below.
WORLD_GLOBE.ISS_COMPASS_NO_SENSOR_STEP1: Use a smartphone (Android or iPhone), not a desktop computer.
WORLD_GLOBE.ISS_COMPASS_NO_SENSOR_STEP2: Open the app over HTTPS (secure connection).
WORLD_GLOBE.ISS_COMPASS_NO_SENSOR_STEP3: On iPhone: allow motion sensor access if prompted.
WORLD_GLOBE.ISS_COMPASS_NO_SENSOR_STEP4: Move the phone in a figure-8 pattern to calibrate the magnetometer, then reopen the compass.
WORLD_GLOBE.ISS_COMPASS_NO_SENSOR_FALLBACK: Meanwhile, North is shown at the top — read the ISS bearing below.
WORLD_GLOBE.ISS_COMPASS_SENSOR_LIVE_HINT: Rotate the phone: alpha, beta and gamma should change.
WORLD_GLOBE.ISS_COMPASS_CAL_NEEDED: North not set — direction may be wrong
WORLD_GLOBE.ISS_COMPASS_CAL_METHOD_AUTO_HINT: The phone finds North on its own
WORLD_GLOBE.ISS_COMPASS_CAL_METHOD_MANUAL_HINT: Point the top of the phone to North
WORLD_GLOBE.ISS_COMPASS_CAL_MANUAL_STEP1: Move away from metal objects and magnets.
WORLD_GLOBE.ISS_COMPASS_CAL_MANUAL_STEP2: Turn so the top of the phone points to North.
WORLD_GLOBE.ISS_COMPASS_CAL_MANUAL_STEP3: When the top aims at North, tap Save.
WORLD_GLOBE.ISS_COMPASS_CAL_DONE_AUTO: North handled by the phone sensors
WORLD_GLOBE.ISS_COMPASS_CAL_SAVED: Setting saved — no need to recalibrate every time
WORLD_GLOBE.ISS_COMPASS_CAL_STEP1: Move away from metal objects and magnets.
WORLD_GLOBE.ISS_COMPASS_CAL_STEP2: Hold the phone flat, top edge forward.
WORLD_GLOBE.ISS_COMPASS_CAL_STEP3: Tap, then walk a few steps in a straight line.
WORLD_GLOBE.ISS_COMPASS_CAL_WALKING: Calibrating… walk in a straight line
WORLD_GLOBE.ISS_COMPASS_CAL_MOVE_MORE: Walk a bit faster, in a straight line
WORLD_GLOBE.ISS_COMPASS_CAL_SUN_STEP1: Move away from metal objects and magnets.
WORLD_GLOBE.ISS_COMPASS_CAL_SUN_STEP2: Hold the phone flat and point the top edge at the Sun.
WORLD_GLOBE.ISS_COMPASS_CAL_SUN_STEP3: When the top edge aims at the Sun, tap Confirm.
WORLD_GLOBE.ISS_COMPASS_CAL_SUN_NIGHT: Sun below horizon — use the GPS walk.
WORLD_GLOBE.ISS_COMPASS_USING_PICKED: Using the point selected on the globe (no GPS).
WORLD_GLOBE.ISS_COMPASS_EXPLAIN: Turn until the shuttle arrow points straight up, then tilt your gaze up by the look-up angle to aim at the ISS.
WORLD_GLOBE.TITLE: Earth globe
WORLD_GLOBE.LIGHT_UNIFORM_HINT: Even illumination across the entire globe.
WORLD_GLOBE.SUBTITLE: Interactive 3D globe (WebGL). Imagery is proxied through the PatTool API; place search uses the same backend as Address/GPS.
WORLD_GLOBE.AUTO_ROTATE_DISABLED_WHILE_ISS_CENTERED: Unavailable while “Keep globe centered on ISS” is on.
WORLD_GLOBE.AUTO_ROTATE_DISABLED_WHILE_FLIGHT: Unavailable while tracking a flight (globe follows the aircraft).
WORLD_GLOBE.HINT_DRAG: Drag to orbit · scroll to zoom · left-click Earth without dragging sets current GPS · right-drag to pan
WORLD_GLOBE.ROADMAP_HINT: Next steps: weather imagery (infrared / clouds), optional satellite basemaps.
WORLD_GLOBE.TEXTURE_LOAD_ERROR: Could not load globe textures via the PatTool API. Ensure the backend is running and reachable, and try again.
WORLD_GLOBE.ISS_ALERT_ENABLED: E-mail alert for visible ISS passes
WORLD_GLOBE.ISS_ALERT_HINT: Receive an e-mail about 30 minutes before the ISS becomes visible over the chosen place.
WORLD_GLOBE.ISS_ALERT_LEAD_NOTE: An e-mail is sent about {{minutes}} min before each visible pass.
WORLD_GLOBE.ISS_ALERT_SAVE_ERROR: Could not save the alert settings.
WORLD_GLOBE.ISS_ALERT_TEST_SENT: Test e-mail sent for the next pass.
WORLD_GLOBE.ISS_ALERT_TEST_NO_PASS: No upcoming visible pass found for this place.
WORLD_GLOBE.ISS_ALERT_TEST_NO_PLACE: Set a place first (city or country).
WORLD_GLOBE.FLIGHT_INTRO: Track a live flight and draw its trajectory on the globe (OpenSky Network).
WORLD_GLOBE.FLIGHT_MODE_HINT: Search by radio call sign / flight number, or by ICAO 24-bit address.
WORLD_GLOBE.FLIGHT_CLEAR_HINT: Stop tracking and forget the saved flight
WORLD_GLOBE.FLIGHT_NOT_FOUND: Flight not found or not currently broadcasting.
WORLD_GLOBE.FLIGHT_OPENSKY_UNAVAILABLE: OpenSky unavailable or rate limit exceeded. Retry in 1–2 minutes, or configure opensky.client-id / opensky.client-secret on the server.
WORLD_GLOBE.FLIGHT_ERROR: Could not reach the flight feed.
WORLD_GLOBE.LIGHT_REALISTIC_HINT: On by default: marked day and night zones from the real Sun (UTC). Off: you can use even globe lighting below.
WORLD_GLOBE.LIGHT_TERMINATOR_NOTE: Uniform lighting is turned off while live day/night is enabled.
WORLD_GLOBE.LIGHT_BOOST_HINT: Light intensities and tone-mapping exposure. Shown value is a multiplier (×1 = baseline for each mode).
WORLD_GLOBE.LIGHT_FROM_USER_HINT: Places the directional sun above your position (geolocation). Until a fix is available, a France fallback is used.
WORLD_GLOBE.LIGHT_FROM_USER_DISABLED_BY_SUN: Turn off “live day/night” to use lighting from your position again.
WORLD_GLOBE.LIGHT_FROM_USER_NO_API: Geolocation is not available in this browser; lighting uses a France fallback.
WORLD_GLOBE.LIGHT_FROM_USER_FALLBACK: No position yet (denied or timeout): temporary lighting centered on France. Use “Refresh my position”.
WORLD_GLOBE.WEATHER_FAILED: Satellite overlay could not be loaded (NASA layer or backend error).
WORLD_GLOBE.LAYER_COUNTRY_BORDERS_HINT: Land-boundary linework from Natural Earth (public domain). Fetched via /api/external/globe/geojson/ne-110m-boundaries-land.
WORLD_GLOBE.LAYER_ISS_HINT: International Space Station sub-point via Open Notify, proxied at /api/external/globe/iss/now. Refreshes on a configurable interval (default 2 s) while enabled; use the numeric field beside the switch.
WORLD_GLOBE.ISS_POLL_INTERVAL_HINT: Seconds between two ISS position requests (2–600). Applies after you leave the field or press Enter.
WORLD_GLOBE.ISS_COUNTDOWN_HINT: Time until the next ISS position request.
WORLD_GLOBE.ISS_REFRESH_NOW_HINT: Fetch ISS position immediately; the countdown restarts using your configured interval.
WORLD_GLOBE.ISS_KEEP_EARTH_CENTERED_HINT: Recenters the view on the ISS ground track each frame (your zoom is kept). Temporarily disables slow auto-rotate while active. Drag with the left mouse button to orbit freely in 3D; toggle this option off and on again or use Reset view to resume tracking.
WORLD_GLOBE.ISS_KEEP_EARTH_CENTERED_DISABLED_PLACE: Unavailable while a place is selected (city or country search). Click elsewhere on Earth or reset the view to clear the place marker.
WORLD_GLOBE.ISS_TRACE_SHOW_HINT: Master switch that shows or hides all the orange ISS track on the globe at once (both the real-time trail and the historical trace). No data is deleted — the lines are only hidden and reappear when switched back on.
WORLD_GLOBE.ISS_TRACE_DISABLED_FLIGHT: ISS trace hidden while tracking a flight.
WORLD_GLOBE.ISS_CENTER_DISABLED_FLIGHT: ISS centering disabled while tracking a flight.
WORLD_GLOBE.ISS_HISTORICAL_TRACE_HINT: Shows the ISS ground track stored on the PatTool server (MongoDB) for the configured retention period (default 1 month). New samples are recorded at most once per minute while the ISS position layer is active.
WORLD_GLOBE.ISS_FORECAST_TRAIL_APPROX: Red line: approximate ISS path for the next hour (forecast API unavailable).
WORLD_GLOBE.ISS_HISTORICAL_TRACE_DATES_HINT: Shows date and time labels along the historical ISS trace, spaced at least 1 minute apart (matches server storage). Off by default.
WORLD_GLOBE.ISS_OLDEST_TRACE_HINT: Date and time of the oldest stored ISS trace point.
WORLD_GLOBE.ISS_HISTORICAL_TRACE_CLEAR_HINT: Delete all stored ISS trace samples from the server and remove the trace from the globe.
WORLD_GLOBE.ISS_BACKGROUND_TRACE_HINT: When enabled, PatTool stores the ISS position in MongoDB every {{minutes}} minutes even if nobody has the globe page open. Setting is saved on the server.
WORLD_GLOBE.ISS_TRACE_DISPLAY_LIMIT_HINT: When enabled (default), the historical ISS trace is reduced to {{points}} points for display. Disable to show every stored point. Setting is saved on the server.
WORLD_GLOBE.BORDERS_FAILED: Boundaries layer could not be loaded (upstream or GeoJSON error).
WORLD_GLOBE.ISS_FETCH_FAILED: ISS position unavailable (upstream or parsing error).
WORLD_GLOBE.ISS_LIVE_STANDARD_HINT: Open Destination Orbite live stream from the space station (new browser tab).
WORLD_GLOBE.ISS_LIVE_HD_HINT: Open Destination Orbite HD live stream from the ISS (new browser tab).
WORLD_GLOBE.ISS_LIVE_PIP_ENABLED_HINT: Show the NASA live feed (same YouTube source as Destination Orbite) in a small panel at the bottom right of the globe. Off by default.
WORLD_GLOBE.ISS_LIVE_PIP_HD_ENABLED_HINT: Show the NASA HD live feed (same YouTube source as Destination Orbite HD page) at the bottom right. On by default.
WORLD_GLOBE.ISS_TICKER_ENABLED_HINT: Shows a stock-style ticker with ISS latitude, longitude, altitude and speed.
WORLD_GLOBE.ISS_LIVE_PIP_RESIZE_HINT: Drag the top-left handle to resize (window stays anchored at the bottom right).
WORLD_GLOBE.ISS_LIVE_PIP_CLOSE_HINT: Close this window (re-enable the stream in the options panel).
WORLD_GLOBE.ISS_FS_SPLIT_RESIZE_HINT: Drag horizontally to adjust ISS stream width vs the globe (fullscreen).
WORLD_GLOBE.ISS_PIP_STACK_RESIZE_HINT: Drag vertically to adjust the top stream height vs the bottom one.
WORLD_GLOBE.ISS_LIVE_PIP_COPY_SCREENSHOT_HINT: Copy only this window’s video area (no toolbar or globe). If prompted, share this tab — only the stream frame is captured.
WORLD_GLOBE.ISS_LIVE_PIP_SHARE_WHATSAPP_HINT: Share on WhatsApp only this window’s video area (no toolbar or globe) plus the live stream link.
WORLD_GLOBE.ISS_LIVE_PIP_WHATSAPP_PASTE_IMAGE: Screenshot copied to clipboard — paste the image in WhatsApp (Ctrl+V).
WORLD_GLOBE.SHARE_WHATSAPP_HINT: Screenshot of the globe and ISS live videos. Like the assistant: preview then Share → WhatsApp (image only).
WORLD_GLOBE.SHARE_WHATSAPP_PREVIEW_HINT: Check the preview, then “Share now”: pick WhatsApp from the system share menu.
WORLD_GLOBE.SHARE_WHATSAPP_NATIVE_NOTE: On mobile: one capture permission (this tab); the view may scroll across each ISS video, then Share → WhatsApp. On PC: image copied — WhatsApp Web, Ctrl+V.
WORLD_GLOBE.SHARE_WHATSAPP_PASTE_HINT: Image copied — open WhatsApp (or WhatsApp Web), open a chat and paste the image (Ctrl+V).
WORLD_GLOBE.SHARE_WHATSAPP_ERROR: Could not share — allow screen capture or share / clipboard in your browser.
WORLD_GLOBE.LAYER_COASTLINES_HINT: Ocean–land shoreline from Natural Earth (public domain). Fetched via /api/external/globe/geojson/ne-110m-coastline.
WORLD_GLOBE.COASTLINE_FAILED: Coastline layer could not be loaded (upstream or GeoJSON error).
WORLD_GLOBE.LAYER_GRATICULE_HINT: Synthetic meridian and parallel ticks every 15° for orientation (no remote data).
WORLD_GLOBE.LAYER_POLITICAL_MAP_HINT: Natural Earth admin‑0 polygons (110m): fan-filled on the sphere, proxied at /api/external/globe/geojson/ne-110m-admin-0-countries. Overlays your basemap.
WORLD_GLOBE.LAYER_COUNTRY_LABELS_HINT: Names at Natural Earth label longitudes/latitudes; follows UI language when NAME_<lang> is available.
WORLD_GLOBE.LAYER_GEOGRAPHIC_LINES_HINT: Major parallels and meridians from Natural Earth (110 m), proxied at /api/external/globe/geojson/ne-110m-geographic-lines.
WORLD_GLOBE.LAYER_RIVERS_HINT: Hydrology linework at 1:50m (many more rivers than 110m). Proxied at /api/external/globe/geojson/ne-50m-rivers-lake-centerlines. Smallest streams are still omitted (not an OSM-level river map).
WORLD_GLOBE.LAYER_LAKES_HINT: Lake polygons at 1:10m (includes large/regional lakes such as Léman). Semi-transparent fill. Proxied at /api/external/globe/geojson/ne-10m-lakes. Very small ponds may still be omitted.
WORLD_GLOBE.LAYER_GLACIERS_HINT: Ice / glacier polygons (110 m), proxied at /api/external/globe/geojson/ne-110m-glaciated-areas.
WORLD_GLOBE.LAYER_CITIES_HINT: Simple points from Natural Earth populated places (110 m), proxied at /api/external/globe/geojson/ne-110m-populated-places-simple. No city name labels.
WORLD_GLOBE.LAYER_TIME_ZONES_HINT: Natural Earth time zones (10 m) coloured by zone; heavy layer. Proxied at /api/external/globe/geojson/ne-10m-time-zones.
WORLD_GLOBE.NATURAL_EARTH_VECTOR_EXTRA_ATTRIBUTION: Natural Earth · vector layers (various scales) · public domain
WORLD_GLOBE.GEOGRAPHIC_LINES_FAILED: Geographic lines could not be loaded (upstream or GeoJSON error).
WORLD_GLOBE.RIVERS_FAILED: Rivers layer could not be loaded (upstream or GeoJSON error).
WORLD_GLOBE.LAKES_FAILED: Lakes layer could not be loaded (upstream or GeoJSON error).

WORLD_GLOBE.CITIES_FAILED: Populated places could not be loaded (upstream or GeoJSON error).

WORLD_GLOBE.ADMIN0_FAILED: Country map GeoJSON could not be loaded.
WORLD_GLOBE.ADMIN0_ATTRIBUTION: Natural Earth · admin‑0 countries 110 m · public domain
WORLD_GLOBE.PICK_POSITION_INVITE: Click land or ocean (tap without dragging) to set latitude / longitude.
WORLD_GLOBE.DETAIL_MAP_TITLE: Detailed map — last picked point or globe center
WORLD_GLOBE.DETAIL_MAP_HINT: A quick click on Earth records GPS coordinates (shown at bottom right). The 2D map opens at that spot when set; otherwise at the middle of your 3D view. Zoom closer for a tighter map zoom.
WORLD_GLOBE.DETAIL_MAP_OSM_NOTE: Map tiles are loaded from OpenStreetMap (not via PatTool).
WORLD_GLOBE.DETAIL_MAP_PICK_FAIL: Rotate the globe so the land you want is centered in the view, then try again.
WORLD_GLOBE.DATA_VIA_BACKEND: Terrain, clouds and satellite imagery are fetched by the server via /api/external/globe (no direct browser calls to NASA or Three.js).

WORLD_GLOBE.ISS_PASS_AMBIGUOUS_HINT: Several places match — pick one for ISS pass times.

LOCAL_NETWORK.ACCESS_DENIED: Access denied. Only administrators can access this feature.
LOCAL_NETWORK.SYNC_MAC_FROM_ARP_TOOLTIP: Replace the stored MAC with the address detected on the network (ARP).
LOCAL_NETWORK.SYNC_MAC_NO_MAPPING: No device mapping for this IP. Add the device to the inventory first.
LOCAL_NETWORK.SYNC_MAC_ERROR: Could not update the MAC address.
LOCAL_NETWORK.VIEW_INVENTORY_ENTRY_TOOLTIP: Open the MongoDB inventory record for this device
LOCAL_NETWORK.INVENTORY_NOT_MAPPED_BODY: No inventory row matches this IP or MAC yet.
LOCAL_NETWORK.INVENTORY_PREVIEW_FORBIDDEN: Administrator role required to load inventory.
LOCAL_NETWORK.EXTRA_INFO_TOGGLE_TOOLTIP: OS, web interface, database, file sharing, remote access, last seen
LOCAL_NETWORK.DEVICE_DETAILS_TOGGLE_TOOLTIP: Vulnerabilities, vendor, services, open ports, and other scan details
LOCAL_NETWORK.VULNERABILITY_TOTAL_BADGE_HINT: {{total}} vulnerabilities across all scanned devices (critical: {{critical}}, high: {{high}})
LOCAL_NETWORK.NO_SCAN_YET: No scan yet. Click « Scan » to begin.
LOCAL_NETWORK.OPEN_DEVICE: Open device in a new tab
LOCAL_NETWORK.OPEN_DETECTED_TOOLTIP: Open detected web interface (HTTP/HTTPS based on ports)
LOCAL_NETWORK.WIFI_SCAN_BTN_TOOLTIP: List Wi‑Fi networks as seen by the PatTool backend host with signal strength.
LOCAL_NETWORK.WIFI_SCAN_EMPTY: No Wi‑Fi networks returned. Ensure the backend host has an active WLAN adapter (nmcli on Linux / netsh on Windows).
LOCAL_NETWORK.WIFI_SCAN_NOTICE: Networks below are measured from the WLAN radio on the machine running PatTool ("{{host}}"), not from your browser or your PC.
LOCAL_NETWORK.WIFI_SCAN_TOGGLE_TITLE: Switch between browser-side link hints vs Access Point enumeration on PatTool backend.
LOCAL_NETWORK.WIFI_SCAN_SOURCE_HINT: PatTool backend probes that host's Wi‑Fi. Browser mode reads only sandboxed Navigator APIs—the same restriction applies when you browse from a phone or tablet.
LOCAL_NETWORK.WIFI_BROWSER_LIMIT_TITLE: No full Wi‑Fi scan from the browser
LOCAL_NETWORK.WIFI_BROWSER_LIMIT_BODY: Whether you browse from a desktop PC, tablet, or smartphone, PatTool still runs inside the browser sandbox — web pages cannot list nearby SSIDs due to privacy and platform restrictions. Pick PatTool backend for a BSSID sweep. Frontend mode exposes at best Network Information hints for the active link.
LOCAL_NETWORK.WIFI_BROWSER_NATIVE_OPTION: To scan WLAN directly from handset firmware you would need a native app layer with OS Wi‑Fi permissions (Capacitor, Kotlin/Swift)—not achievable from Angular inside the browser.
LOCAL_NETWORK.WIFI_BROWSER_NO_NI_API: Network Information API (navigator.connection) is unavailable — no browser-side link hints.
LOCAL_NETWORK.WIFI_BROWSER_REFRESH_HINT: Click Refresh — the table may also update when the browser fires a Network Information change.
LOCAL_NETWORK.WIFI_NATIVE_ANDROID_LIST_INTRO: Networks sampled on-device using the bundled PatTool/Capacitor Android shell (WLAN scan RSSI whenever the OS exposes results).
LOCAL_NETWORK.WIFI_NATIVE_IOS_LIST_INTRO: WLAN details for the currently connected SSID supplied by iOS (full neighbour scan is constrained on iOS versus Android).
LOCAL_NETWORK.WIFI_NATIVE_PERM_DENIED: Location/Wi‑Fi permission denied — on Android grant location access (Wi‑Fi scans reuse it), then reopen the scanner.
LOCAL_NETWORK.WIFI_NATIVE_ANDROID_EMPTY: No APs detected or scan throttled by Android (radios/off or OS limits). Toggle Wi‑Fi and retry.
LOCAL_NETWORK.WIFI_NATIVE_IOS_NO_DATA: iOS did not return WLAN info — check the supplementary browser metrics below.
LOCAL_NETWORK.WIFI_CAPACITOR_PACKAGING_HINT: Full phone-side surveys require a packaged Android/iOS app (run npm run cap:sync and open Android Studio/Xcode). Visiting ng serve alone cannot access OS Wi‑Fi scanners.
LOCAL_NETWORK.SHOW_ONLY_MAC_CONFLICT_TOOLTIP: Show only devices where the detected MAC (ARP) differs from the MongoDB inventory MAC
LOCAL_NETWORK.MAC_CONFLICT_COUNT_TOOLTIP: Devices whose network MAC differs from the inventory record
LOCAL_NETWORK.CREATE_LAN_PROXY_HINT: Open IoT LAN proxies and add an entry with this device’s upstream URL

LOTTO.TITLE: French Loto (FDJ) draws
LOTTO.INTRO: Draws come from the server MongoDB: they load automatically when you open this page; you can also use “Reload table” anytime. Pick a start and end month (inclusive), then run import — by default the end is the current calendar month (capped at the archive’s last month when needed) and the start is the previous month. Archive coverage is October 2008 through May 2026. Administrators only.
LOTTO.INVALID_RANGE: Start month must be before or equal to end month.
LOTTO.FILTER_EMPTY: No draws match this date range.
LOTTO.EMPTY: No draws in the database yet; an admin can run the import.
LOTTO.LOAD_ERROR: Could not load draws from the server.
LOTTO.LOGIN_REQUIRED: Sign in to start the import (the server will crawl the archives for several minutes).
LOTTO.SYNC_ADMIN_ONLY: Archive import is restricted to accounts with the Administrator (Admin) role.
LOTTO.SYNC_ADMIN_TOOLTIP: Only users with the Admin role can run this import.
LOTTO.SYNC_DONE: Import finished: {{months}} months processed, {{draws}} draws saved (HTTP errors: {{errors}}).
LOTTO.DATE_SAVE_FORBIDDEN: Saving is restricted to administrators (or your session expired).
LOTTO.DATE_EDIT_TOOLTIP: Turn draw date editing on or off (admin). Dates are read-only until you start editing.
LOTTO.JSON_AI_TOOLTIP: Assistant: pre-aggregated `pat-loto-ai-v2` (counts + tail). Export modal keeps full recordCount/draws.
LOTTO.JSON_AI_MODAL_HINT: Readable export: root recordCount, draws[] (drawDate, numbers, chance). The assistant button sends precomputed histogram JSON (smaller prompt).
LOTTO.AI_FAB_TOOLTIP: Opens the assistant with the **new quantitative prompt** + **pre-aggregated** statistics JSON (`pat-loto-ai-v2`). You send the message when ready.
LOTTO.AI_PROMPT_FROM_SCRATCH: **French Lotto — human-readable answer.** Data = **`pat-loto-ai-v2`** below (UI pre-counted histograms): **`c`={{n}}**, `mf` for mains **1–49**, `cf` for Chance **1–10**, **`chi2*Naive`**, **`tail`**.** Mandatory order:** **(1) First lines must be grids** — **GRID_A** then **GRID_B**, each **one readable line**: **five ascending numbers + Chance**, e.g. `Numbers: 4, 11, 22, 35, 41 — Chance: 7`. Then **one sentence**: model fit to the JSON, not the official draw. **(2) Short method:** **maximum five** bullet lines, each ≤one short sentence (**no** sub‑paragraphs or nested headings). **(3) Fair play** — one sentence.** Presentation:** PatTool renders **Markdown** — use a `##` heading for the grids block; **bold** every number inside **GRID_A** / **GRID_B**; separate sections with `---`; use `-` bullet lines for the method; put the lottery disclaimer in a `>` blockquote (renders tinted). Optionally a **tiny** GFM table (header plus **only** the two slips); **never** a large statistical table.** Do not** paste raw `<html>` / `style=` (**stripped by the app**, so colours come from Markdown structure alone). **Still forbidden:** dumping every main number with stats; long maths **before** the grids; decorative RNG.
LOTTO.AI_STATS_PAYLOAD_INTRO: **Attached aggregate JSON (`pat-loto-ai-v2`)** — reason from histograms plus `tail`, not kilometric per-draw transcripts.
LOTTO.AI_JSON_BLOCK_INTRO: Compact JSON (fewer tokens): `c` = authoritative count = **`d.length`**. Each `d[i]` = `[ "YYYYMMDD", [5 numbers], chance ]` chronological.
LOTTO.AI_RECORD_COUNT_LINE: Authoritative count: **{{n}}** (= JSON `c`; must equal **`d.length`**). Do not infer from how many tuple rows you see — long JSON can be truncated. If `c`≠visible `d` length, say so and analyse only the visible slice.
LOTTO.AI_MESSAGE_1: Lotto draws in **compact** JSON below. Trust **`c`** and the bold line; use visible `d` tuples. If context is partial, say so. First apply the **most advanced statistics & probability** you reasonably can: marginals/joints for five numbers + Chance, entropy, spacing & rank summaries, cautious serial checks; **goodness-of-fit** vs fair-draw nulls (multinomial, etc.), **bootstrap / permutation**, **multiplicity control** when stacking tests; derive **combinatorial** win odds under official rules. **Show your work**: name each model/hypothesis and give **reproducible detail**—explicit formulas, aggregated counts from the visible history, test statistics and **p**-values / intervals (or bootstrap/permutation bands where used), hyperparameters (e.g. Dirichlet α), and optimisation objective + constraints for each constructed slip. **Then**, as a compulsory final section (e.g. “Model-derived play slips”), output **≥2 concrete candidate slips** (five numbers + Chance) that are **not** picked at random—each slip must cite how the numbers follow from the computations above (not a black box). Optionally a rough calibrated comparison to uniformity. Acknowledge briefly that in an ideal fair lottery no past-data estimator improves **intrinsic** win probability; slips are **explicit outputs** of your models, not claims of prophecy.
LOTTO.CHART_MODAL_HELP: For each calendar month: mean of the 5 numbers (after ascending sort: rank 1 = smallest drawn … rank 5 = largest), and mean of the Chance number — if several draws occur in the same month, averages pool all of them.
LOTTO.CHART_EMPTY: Not enough data to plot the chart (missing draws or numbers).
LOTTO.SOURCE_NOTE: Source: public data aggregated by LesBonsNumeros.com (not affiliated with FDJ). Verify on fdj.fr if needed.

EUROMILLIONS.AI_FLOW_INFO_BTN_TOOLTIP: How EuroMillions prepares the assistant message
EUROMILLIONS.AI_FLOW_INFO_BTN_ARIA: Open help: EuroMillions assistant message and JSON data
EUROMILLIONS.AI_FLOW_INFO_MODAL_TITLE: EuroMillions — how the assistant message is built
EUROMILLIONS.AI_FLOW_INFO_LEAD: PatTool fills the assistant input with a ready-to-edit message: instructions for the model, a short recap of the analysis angle(s), and a compact JSON block (schema pat-eurom-ai-v2). Adjust the options below, then click the main button.
EUROMILLIONS.AI_FLOW_INFO_S1_BODY: Opens the assistant panel with that message already inserted (new conversation; nothing is sent until you press Send in the assistant). You must be signed in and have draws in the table. If the button stays disabled, check login and loading.
EUROMILLIONS.AI_FLOW_INFO_S2_BODY: Unchecked: standard scratch prompt and only one method — the one selected with the radio on the cards. Checked: multi-method synthesis prompt and several method specs can be attached to the JSON (using the checkboxes under each card). This checkbox alone does not open the assistant.
EUROMILLIONS.AI_FLOW_INFO_S3_BODY: The radio always picks the primary angle. Its specification is copied to the JSON root fields (calculationMethodId, label, summary, spec). With several methods attached in synthesis mode, the radio still marks which one is primary for those root fields.
EUROMILLIONS.AI_FLOW_INFO_S4_BODY: Only enabled when multi-method synthesis is on. They decide which detailed specs are listed in calculationMethods in the JSON. At least one must stay checked so the main button works in synthesis mode. When synthesis is off, they are greyed out and only the radio-selected method is included in the JSON.
EUROMILLIONS.AI_FLOW_INFO_NOTE: Draws included in the JSON start from the configured minimum date (currently {{since}}). Admins can change it in the panel above the action toolbar.
EUROMILLIONS.METHOD_RATING_ARIA: PatTool usefulness hint for this analysis angle: {{score}} out of {{max}} stars (not a statistical proof or prediction).
EUROMILLIONS.METHOD_RECOMPUTE_HINT: Recomputes all five analytics blocks in MongoDB for the current draw window.
EUROMILLIONS.METHOD_CHI2_GOF_UNIFORM_DESC: Pearson χ² on pooled main-ball counts (50 bins, 5×n slots) plus per-era star grids matching FDJ star ranges.
EUROMILLIONS.METHOD_CHI2_GOF_UNIFORM_SUMMARY: Pearson χ² comparing observed counts to uniform draws (main balls + stars per FDJ era).
EUROMILLIONS.METHOD_ENTROPY_NORMALIZED_DESC: Empirical entropy H for mains and stars divided by log(K) — measures dispersion vs uniform max-entropy.
EUROMILLIONS.METHOD_ENTROPY_NORMALIZED_SUMMARY: How spread empirical frequencies are vs uniform (normalized entropy on mains and stars).
EUROMILLIONS.METHOD_GAP_RECURRENCE_DESC: Per ball 1–50, mean spacing between draw indices where it appears; summarizes heterogeneity of recurrence times.
EUROMILLIONS.METHOD_GAP_RECURRENCE_SUMMARY: Average spacing between consecutive appearances of the same main ball.
EUROMILLIONS.METHOD_SUM_CORRELATION_TITLE: Σ mains vs Σ stars correlation
EUROMILLIONS.METHOD_SUM_CORRELATION_DESC: Pearson r between the sum of the five main numbers and the sum of the two stars on draws with complete valid grids.
EUROMILLIONS.METHOD_SUM_CORRELATION_SUMMARY: Linear link between the sum of five mains and the sum of two stars (Pearson correlation).
EUROMILLIONS.METHOD_MONTE_CARLO_MAXFREQ_DESC: Compares observed maximum main-ball frequency to simulations under uniform draws without replacement; empirical p-value.
EUROMILLIONS.METHOD_MONTE_CARLO_MAXFREQ_SUMMARY: Compare the most frequent main ball to random simulations (empirical p-value).
EUROMILLIONS.METHOD_CHI2_GOF_UNIFORM_AI_SPEC: BINDING METHOD (user choice: χ² naïve uniform). Scope = JSON `sinceInclusive` / `tail` / `periods` only. (1) Main balls 1–50: let n = number of draws in scope, total slots = 5n. For each k∈{1,…,50}, count O_k = appearances of k across all five positions of all draws (order within a draw irrelevant). Expected E = 5n/50 each. Pearson χ²_main = Σ_k (O_k−E)²/E with df = 49. Use PatTool pre-counts in `periods[].mf` / `chi2MainNaive` as cross-check but recompute from `tail` if you show detail. (2) Stars by FDJ era: assign each draw to period P1/P2/P3 using `periodCuts` and JSON `periods[]` starMax (9 / 11 / 12). Within period j with n_j draws and starMax S_j, pool 2n_j star-slots; counts O_s for s∈{1,…,S_j}; expected E_j = 2n_j/S_j each; χ²_stars,j = Σ_s (O_s−E_j)²/E_j with df = S_j−1. State hypotheses explicitly (uniform marginal over slots within era). Do not replace this by generic multinomial-without-replacement theory unless clearly labeled extra; primary deliverable = these χ² statistics + df + plain interpretation.
EUROMILLIONS.METHOD_ENTROPY_NORMALIZED_AI_SPEC: BINDING METHOD (user choice: normalized Shannon entropy). Scope = draws in `tail` aligned with `periods`. Natural log. (1) Mains: empirical p_k = (count of ball k in pooled 5n slots)/(5n), k=1..50. H_main = −Σ p_k ln p_k (0 ln 0 = 0). H_norm_main = H_main / ln(50) ∈ [0,1]; equals 1 iff uniform on categories. (2) Stars per era j: categories 1..S_j, total slots 2n_j, same formula H_j; H_norm_j = H_j / ln(S_j). Report numeric H and H_norm; interpret as deviation from max-entropy baseline; optional comparison to uniform benchmark.
EUROMILLIONS.METHOD_GAP_RECURRENCE_AI_SPEC: BINDING METHOD (user choice: inter-draw recurrence gaps). Index draws in chronological order as `tail` (index t=0..n−1). For each ball b∈{1,…,50}, list indices T_b where b appears (≤1 per draw). If |T_b|≥2, gaps Δ_i = T_b[i]−T_b[i−1]; mean gap ḡ_b = mean(Δ). Summarize over balls with ≥2 appearances: e.g. mean of ḡ_b, spread (sd/min/max). Descriptive only; links to renewal/inter-arrival intuition allowed but no forecast claim. Ignore balls with <2 hits for ḡ_b.
EUROMILLIONS.METHOD_SUM_CORRELATION_AI_SPEC: BINDING METHOD (user choice: Pearson correlation of sums). Consider draws where there are exactly five main integers in [1,50] and two star integers in [1,starMax] for that draw’s regulatory era (derive starMax from date vs `periodCuts`). Let X = sum of five mains, Y = sum of two stars. Pearson r between X and Y across all qualifying draws; report sample size n_used. State formula r = cov(X,Y)/(σ_X σ_Y). No causal interpretation; mention linear association only.
EUROMILLIONS.METHOD_MONTE_CARLO_MAXFREQ_AI_SPEC: BINDING METHOD (user choice: Monte Carlo calibration of max pooled frequency). From `tail`, pooled main counts over 5n slots; M_obs = max_{k=1..50} count(k). Null operational model for simulation: independent draws, each draw picks 5 distinct mains uniformly from {1,…,50} without replacement across draws’ sampling mechanism is approximated by repeating n independent such draws; replicate R≥800 times; each replicate yields M_sim; empirical one-sided p-value = (#(M_sim ≥ M_obs)+1)/(R+1). State R and that procedure; if you vary R, say so. Not a proof of bias—interpret as descriptive Monte Carlo tail probability.
EUROMILLIONS.AI_SELECTED_METHOD_HINT: **Normative methodology:** The user selected **`{{label}}`** (`{{id}}`). You MUST treat **`calculationMethodSpec`** in the JSON as the **binding operational definition** of that choice (not just the short summary). Apply it step-by-step when reasoning on `tail` / `periods`. Quick reminder: {{summary}} — Data: all draws since `sinceInclusive` appear in `tail` (`tail.length` = `c`); aggregates in `periods`. Echo fields `calculationMethodId`, `calculationMethodLabel`, `calculationMethodSummary` only as labels; **`calculationMethodSpec` overrides ambiguity.**
EUROMILLIONS.AI_FAB_TOOLTIP: **EuroMillions**: scratch prompt + **`pat-eurom-ai-v2`** (aggregates + **all** draws since {{since}} in `tail`). Send manually.
EUROMILLIONS.AI_PROMPT_FROM_SCRATCH: **EuroMillions — human-readable answer.** Data = **`pat-eurom-ai-v2`** below (PatTool pre-counts): scope **`sinceInclusive` = {{since}}** (server property `euromillions.ai.min-draw-date`); **`c`={{n}}** draws in that scope; **`periods[]`** per era (`mf` for mains **1–50** across **5×n** slots, `sf` for stars up to **starMax**, **`chi2*Naive`**), plus **`tail`** = **every** draw in that scope, chronological tuples `[YYYYMMDD,[5 mains],[★,★]]` (**`tail.length`** = **`c`**).** Mandatory order:** **(1) Lead with grids — zero preamble:** first printable lines must be **GRID_A:** one line, **five ascending mains + two ascending stars**, plain language e.g. `Main balls: 7, 12, 23, 34, 45 — Stars: 3, 9`. Then **GRID_B:** second deterministic line, same pattern. Then **one sentence**: these are **statistical model outputs from the JSON**, not the official next draw. **(2) Short method — max five bullet lines**, each one short sentence (**no** extra sections or long prose). **Forbidden:** dumping rows like `P… ball s=… obs=… smoothed_prob=` for dozens of indices (fills the reply and truncates before the user sees numbers). At most **one** χ² + **df** line if helpful. **(3) Fair play — one sentence:** draws are random; no prophecy.** Presentation:** PatTool renders **Markdown** (`##` / `###`, **bold**, bullets, horizontal rules, blockquotes for colour/tint in the UI). Structure the answer that way; **no** raw HTML/`style=`. A **small** GFM table (header + **only** **GRID_A**/**GRID_B** rows) is fine; **no** huge tables.** Always forbidden:** long numeric traces **before** GRID_A; decorative RNG.
EUROMILLIONS.AI_MULTI_METHOD_HINT: **Multiple methodologies:** If JSON contains **`calculationMethods`** (array), root fields (`calculationMethodId`, `calculationMethodSpec`, …) duplicate the **primary** lens (**`{{primaryLabel}}`**, id **`{{primaryId}}`**). You MUST honor **every** row’s **`calculationMethodSpec`** when commenting on `tail` / `periods`. Tie angles briefly (**{{labels}}**) **without** merging incompatible statistical definitions into one vague paragraph; note tensions honestly.
EUROMILLIONS.AI_MULTI_SYNTHESIS_HINT: **Multi-method synthesis mode:** **`calculationMethods`** lists **`{{n}}`** binding specs (UI order). Run **each** method **only inside its own subsection** per the main prompt **before** cross-method synthesis. Do **not** blur distinct operational definitions; **`{{primaryLabel}}`** (`{{primaryId}}`) anchors GRID reasoning continuity with root duplicate fields.
EUROMILLIONS.AI_PROMPT_MULTI_SYNTHESIS: **EuroMillions — multi-method synthesis (human-readable).** Same data schema **`pat-eurom-ai-v2`** as standard scratch: **`sinceInclusive` = {{since}}**, **`c`={{n}}**, **`periods[]`**, **`tail`** (**`tail.length`** = **`c`**).** Mandatory order:** **(1) Grids first — zero preamble:** **GRID_A** then **GRID_B** as in standard scratch (five ascending mains + two ascending stars each line); one sentence that these are **model outputs**, not the official draw. **(2) Per-method blocks:** For **each** object in JSON **`calculationMethods`** (preserve order), add **`###`** heading = that row’s **`calculationMethodLabel`**, then **max three** `-` bullets applying **only** that row’s **`calculationMethodSpec`** to `tail`/`periods`. One subsection per method; **no** mixing formulas across subsections. If **`calculationMethods`** is absent (single-method payload), treat root **`calculationMethodSpec`** as the only block (still use **`###`** + three bullets max). **(3) Cross-method synthesis:** `## Cross-method synthesis` then **3–5** `-` bullets on agreements, caveats, or non-reconcilable tension (**without** forcing a false reconciliation); **one** cautious sentence: none of this predicts the official draw. **(4) Fair play — one sentence.** Same Markdown/HTML rules as standard scratch (**no** raw HTML/`style=`).
EUROMILLIONS.AI_STATS_PAYLOAD_INTRO: **Attached JSON (`pat-eurom-ai-v2`)** — scope **`sinceInclusive`** (**{{since}}**): use `periods` + `tail` (**full** chronological list for that window; **`tail.length`** = **`c`**).
EUROMILLIONS.AI_JSON_BLOCK_INTRO: Compact JSON (fewer tokens): `c` = authoritative count = **`d.length`**. Each `d[i]` = `[ "YYYYMMDD", [5 main balls], [star1, star2] ]` chronological.
EUROMILLIONS.AI_RECORD_COUNT_LINE: Authoritative count: **{{n}}** (= JSON `c`; should equal **`tail.length`** in this schema). If the model context looks cut off, say so; otherwise **`c`** matches **`tail.length`**.
EUROMILLIONS.AI_MESSAGE_1: EuroMillions draws in **compact** JSON below. Trust **`c`** and the bold line; use visible `d` tuples. If context is partial, say so. First apply **state-of-the-art statistics & probability** on the visible history: empirical laws & dependence structure for mains + stars if testable; entropy/gaps/ranks; **GOF**, **bootstrap/permutation**, **multiplicity-aware** inference; regime changes if periods differ combinatorially. **Show your work**: for every major step give **reproducible calculation detail**—named hypotheses, explicit formulas, tabulated or summarised counts from the data you see, test statistics and **p**-values / CIs (or bootstrap/permutation bands), model hyperparameters, and the exact optimisation or selection rule + objective value that yields each slip. **Then**, compulsory final section (e.g. “Model-derived play slips”), output **≥2 full candidate combinations** (5 mains + 2 stars) constructed **deterministically** from that pipeline—each combination must trace back to those computations (**no** black-box picks). Briefly quantify under your model vs i.i.d. uniform if tractable; one sentence: fair-draw physics means claims of **edge** versus true randomness are usually unwarranted—deliver slips as **explicit outputs** of your estimators nevertheless.

CALCULATOR.TITLE: Calculator
CALCULATOR.SUBTITLE: Standard arithmetic (+, −, ×, ÷). Enable “scientific calculator” for sin, cos, √, logs, π, and more.
CALCULATOR.KEYBOARD_HINT: Keyboard: digits; + − * /; Enter or = for equals; Esc clears all; Backspace deletes the last digit.
CALCULATOR.TAPE_MODE_LABEL: Detailed view (numbers and operators, one line each)
CALCULATOR.TAPE_EMPTY: Each number and operator will appear here.
CALCULATOR.SCIENTIFIC_ANGLES_HINT: Use the switch: trigonometry and inverse trig in degrees or radians.
CALCULATOR.SCI_TITLE_EXP: e raised to the power of x
CALCULATOR.SCI_TITLE_EXP10: 10 raised to the power of x
CALCULATOR.FORMULA_HINT: Use x as the variable. Examples: sin(x), x^2 + 3*x, sqrt(abs(x)), exp(-x^2), log(x, 10). Trigonometry here uses radians (multiply by pi/180 for degrees). Constants: pi, e. Definite integral ∫ₐᵇ f(x) dx uses composite Simpson’s rule (even number of subintervals). Presets labelled ∫… show antiderivatives to plot. Optional purple curve: cumulative ∫ from x min (trapezoids on samples; second Y axis).
CALCULATOR.FORMULA_INTEGRAL_A_ARIA: Lower limit a of definite integral
CALCULATOR.FORMULA_INTEGRAL_B_ARIA: Upper limit b of definite integral
CALCULATOR.FORMULA_INTEGRAL_PANELS_ARIA: Number of subintervals for Simpson rule (adjusted to even)
CALCULATOR.FORMULA_FS_MATH_BAR_ARIA: Buttons to insert math expressions into the formula
CALCULATOR.FORMULA_PLOT_INTEGRAL_HINT: Adds ∫ from x min to x using trapezoids on the same samples as the curve (second vertical axis). Restarts at 0 after gaps where f is undefined.
CALCULATOR.FORMULA_INTEGRAL_CURVE_CAPTION: Purple curve — cumulative ∫ from {{xmin}} to x of ( {{expr}} ), trapezoidal rule.
CALCULATOR.FORMULA_ERR_SYNTAX: Could not parse the expression. Check parentheses and function names.
CALCULATOR.FORMULA_ERR_NONREAL: Result is not a real number at this x (complex or undefined).
CALCULATOR.FORMULA_ERR_RANGE: Invalid interval: x max must be greater than x min.
CALCULATOR.FORMULA_ERR_NOPOINTS: No points could be plotted on this interval (domain errors or non-real values).
CALCULATOR.FORMULA_ERR_INTEGRAL_RANGE: Invalid limits: a and b must be finite with b greater than a.
CALCULATOR.FORMULA_ERR_INTEGRAL_FAIL: Numerical integration failed (non-real or undefined values on [a, b]).

ABOUT.MONGODB_DESC: Cloud NoSQL document database with authentication
ABOUT.DISCUSSION_DESC: Real-time discussion via WebSocket (STOMP/SockJS) with backend persistence (Spring Boot).

ABOUT.REPORT_ACTIONS_SUBTITLE: Send by email, view or refresh the report in a single click.
ABOUT.STREAMING_ARCHITECTURE_DESC: Hybrid streaming system with client-side caching
ABOUT.STREAMING_OVERVIEW: The system uses a hybrid approach: backend streams ALL events once via Server-Sent Events (SSE), frontend caches ALL events in memory, and displays only 8 at a time with client-side pagination.
ABOUT.STREAMING_BACKEND_DESC: Backend streams all matching events from MongoDB 8.2 via SSE, one by one, in real-time.
ABOUT.STREAMING_FRONTEND_DESC: Frontend caches all events in memory. Scrolling uses cached data - NO backend calls.
ABOUT.STREAMING_BENEFITS_DESC: Fast scrolling with no network latency, instant display of next 8 events, offline-ready, instant search/filter on cached data.
ABOUT.STREAMING_APPROACH_DESC: Stream once, cache all, client-side pagination

METEO_FRANCE.TITLE: Precision weather
METEO_FRANCE.SUBTITLE: Météo-France radar, current conditions and forecast
METEO_FRANCE.SHOW_TEMPERATURE_MAP_HINT: Station temperatures via Météo-France DPObs v2 (/station/infrahoraire-6m). Open-Meteo fallback if not configured.
METEO_FRANCE.TEMPERATURE_MAP_HINT: Current temperatures as numbers on the map.
METEO_FRANCE.TEMPERATURE_MAP_HINT_MF: Météo-France station observations (DPObs v2) across the visible map; overlapping labels are hidden. °C/°F per your choice.
METEO_FRANCE.TEMPERATURE_MAP_HINT_OPENMETEO: Open-Meteo fallback (set meteofrance.obs.api.token for Météo-France observations).
METEO_FRANCE.DPOBS_NOT_CONFIGURED: MF observations v2: subscribe to « Public Observation Data » on portail-api.meteofrance.fr, then set meteofrance.obs.api.token (Open-Meteo fallback active).
METEO_FRANCE.DPOBS_INVALID_TOKEN: Météo-France observations token rejected (401). Check meteofrance.obs.api.token (« Public Observation Data » API, distinct from radar and clim keys).
METEO_FRANCE.RADAR_REFRESH_INTERVAL_HINT: Seconds between two radar updates (30–600, default 60). Saved when you leave the field or press Enter.
METEO_FRANCE.RADAR_HINT: Click the map or drag the marker to pick a location. Radar shows precipitation, not clouds.
METEO_FRANCE.RADAR_RAINVIEWER_FALLBACK: (RainViewer radar display proxied by PatTool — MF API returns HDF5 files)
METEO_FRANCE.RADAR_CLEAR_SKY: Dry weather: no radar colors is normal.
METEO_FRANCE.RADAR_LOAD_ERROR: Could not load the radar layer.
METEO_FRANCE.DPRADAR_NOT_CONFIGURED: Radar unavailable: subscribe free to the « Public Radar Data » API on portail-api.meteofrance.fr, then set meteofrance.api.token in application.properties (or PatTool Parameters).
METEO_FRANCE.DPRADAR_INVALID_TOKEN: Météo-France token rejected (401). On the portal: « Public Radar Data » API → Configure → « API Key » → Generate token → paste into meteofrance.api.token (no Bearer prefix). Or use meteofrance.oauth.application-id.
METEO_FRANCE.GPS_UNAVAILABLE: Geolocation is not available on this device.
METEO_FRANCE.CLIM_LOADING: Ordering and downloading archives (may take a few seconds)…
METEO_FRANCE.CLIM_LOAD_ERROR: Could not load climatological data for this period or station.
METEO_FRANCE.CLIM_NO_DATA: No data for the requested period.
METEO_FRANCE.CLIM_HINT: Official DPClim archives (CSV). Requires « Climatological Data » subscription + meteofrance.clim.api.token (different from the radar key).
METEO_FRANCE.DPCLIM_NOT_CONFIGURED: Clim archives unavailable: subscribe to « Données Climatologiques » on portail-api.meteofrance.fr, then set meteofrance.clim.api.token (separate from the radar key).
METEO_FRANCE.DPCLIM_INVALID_TOKEN: DPClim key rejected (401). Portal: « Données Climatologiques » API → API Key → meteofrance.clim.api.token (not the radar key).
